stem: A kite is flying at a height h directly above point A on level ground. A string runs from the kite to a person standing at point B on the ground, making a 45-degree angle with the ground. A second string runs from the kite to an anchor at point C on the ground, on the opposite side of A from B, making a 60-degree angle with the ground. If the distance from B to C is 100 meters, what is h, in meters?
explanation: Two right triangles share the vertical side h (the kite's height above A). In right triangle with B: tan(45) = h/AB, so AB = h. In right triangle with C: tan(60) = h/AC, so AC = h/sqrt(3). Since B and C are on opposite sides of A, BC = AB + AC = h + h/sqrt(3) = h(1 + 1/sqrt(3)) = h(sqrt(3) + 1)/sqrt(3) = 100. Solving: h = 100sqrt(3)/(sqrt(3) + 1). Rationalizing: h = 100sqrt(3)(sqrt(3) - 1)/((sqrt(3))^2 - 1^2) = 100sqrt(3)(sqrt(3) - 1)/2 = 100(3 - sqrt(3))/2 = 50(3 - sqrt(3)). Verification: h = 50(3 - sqrt(3)) approximately equals 63.4. AB = 63.4, AC = 63.4/sqrt(3) approximately equals 36.6. BC = 63.4 + 36.6 = 100. Confirmed.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: Sets up tan(45) = h/AB and tan(60) = h/AC, uses AB + AC = 100, solves h = 100sqrt(3)/(sqrt(3) + 1), and rationalizes to get h = 50(3 - sqrt(3))
distractorRationale.B: Treats BC = 100 as the hypotenuse of a single right triangle with the kite at the top and uses sin(45) = h/100, obtaining h = 100 sin(45) = 100(sqrt(2)/2) = 50sqrt(2), instead of setting up two separate right triangles = 50\sqrt{2}
distractorRationale.C: Inverts the tangent ratio for the 60-degree angle, using tan(60) = AC/h instead of h/AC, which gives AC = h sqrt(3). Then AB + AC = h + h sqrt(3) = h(1 + sqrt(3)) = 100, yielding h = 100/(1 + sqrt(3)) = 50(sqrt(3) - 1) = 50(\sqrt{3} - 1)
distractorRationale.D: Sets up the equations correctly and reaches h = 100sqrt(3)/(sqrt(3) + 1), but when rationalizing the denominator computes (sqrt(3))^2 - 1^2 as 3 + 1 = 4 instead of 3 - 1 = 2, giving h = 100sqrt(3)(sqrt(3) - 1)/4 = 100(3 - sqrt(3))/4 = 25(3 - sqrt(3)) = 25(3 - \sqrt{3})

imageDescription: A vertical dashed line represents the kite's height h above point A on the ground. Point B is to the left of A on a horizontal ground line, and point C is to the right of A. A line from the kite to B makes a 45-degree angle with the ground at B. A line from the kite to C makes a 60-degree angle with the ground at C. The distance BC along the ground is labeled 100 m. Point A is directly below the kite on segment BC.
